7.description: Trading volume on Uniswap. The graph shows the daily volume of transactions including flash loans in USD. The graph is capped at 2.4 billion USD.

9.description: Liquidity withdrawals and extreme market events. Panel A shows the block-by-block price of Ether around May 19, 2020 (in orange, left axis), the aggregate percentage liquidity withdrawals from the four largest ETH-USD stablecoin pools on Uniswap (blue, right axis), and the fee revenue for the liquidity pools in thousand USD (green, far right axis). Panel B depicts the price of Ether and the median gas cost of a liquidity withdrawal in USD.

11.description: Pricing error and pool size. This figure shows the pricing error for the USDC/ETH pair in percent of the Binance price (blue line, right axis) and the pool size of the Uniswap USDC/ETH pool (orange, log-scale, left axis). Prices are calculated at minute intervals. The figure plots these errors sampled every eight hours.

12.description: Intraday prices for the USDC/ETH pair on October 21, 2020. The graph shows minute-by-minute prices of the USDC/ETH pair on Binance and Uniswap.

13.description: Price impact of USDC/ETH on Uniswap (orange, green) and Binance (blue). Price impact is computed as the change in price over volume (green and blue lines) as well as analytically as the price change for a marginal unit bought using the bonding curve formula (green line). Binance price impact is cut off at 0.00001.

14.description: Trading volume of USDC/ETH on Uniswap (orange) and Binance (blue). The graph shows the trading volume excluding flash loans in ETH. Trading volume is aggregated over rolling eight-hour intervals.

D1.description: Network graph of pools between all tokens that are part of the 50 largest pools by total aggregate volume in USD. The size of a node is proportional to the log of the number of pools a token is part of, its color is proportional to the total aggregate liquidity staked for a given token across all the pools, an edge between two tokens indicates that two tokens are in a common pool, and the thickness of the edge is proportional to trading volume.

F1.description: Incremental fees of a swap transaction over two token transfers measured in USD.
